# TramsTec

> TramsTec is **WhatsApp memory and business-context infrastructure for Latin America**. The persistent memory layer that lets AI agents ã Claude, OpenAI Agents, LangChain, Cursor, n8n, and custom ã truly understand and operate LATAM businesses on WhatsApp. Not a messaging API, not a chatbot builder. The infrastructure on top.

## What TramsTec is

TramsTec is the **conversational memory layer** between AI systems and WhatsApp-first businesses in LATAM. Existing messaging APIs (Twilio, MessageBird, the WhatsApp Business API directly) give you a channel. TramsTec assumes that's solved and builds on top what an AI agent actually needs to operate a real business:

- Persistent, per-customer conversational memory
- Automatic CRM enrichment and timeline generation
- Order, lead, and intent extraction from free-form text
- Semantic search over the entire business memory (pgvector)
- Commercial continuity (pending follow-ups, decision history)
- Mexico/LATAM business workflows (SAT, CFDI 4.0, SPEI, OXXO, collections)
- Infrastructure-grade reliability, observability, audit trail
- Multi-tenant isolation, scoped API keys, white-label

## What TramsTec is NOT

- Not an AI agent or chatbot ã you bring your own (any MCP/OpenAI/LangChain/HTTP client).
- Not a simple WhatsApp wrapper ã the WhatsApp Cloud API is upstream plumbing for us, not the product.
- Not a chatbot builder, drag-and-drop tool, or no-code flow editor.
- Not vendor lock-in: every byte of memory is exportable in JSON or Parquet at any time.

## Who it's for

- **AI engineers** building vertical agents that need to operate WhatsApp businesses with real memory.
- **AI-native SaaS** companies that want WhatsApp + LATAM context as a building block, not a project.
- **Agencies** managing multiple WhatsApp clients with one API and per-client scoped keys.
- **LATAM businesses** (retail, services, food, logistics) replacing "human typing replies" with "agent with context".
- **Vertical AI startups** in fintech, healthtech, edtech, retailtech that operate via WhatsApp in Mexico, Argentina, Colombia, Chile, Peru, Brazil.

## Core AI-native tools (semantic, not CRUD)

- `getCustomerContext(phone)` ã Identity, last orders, interests, commercial stage, language, sentiment.
- `getCommercialHistory(phone)` ã Clean timeline of orders, payments, quotes, returns, follow-ups.
- `getPendingFollowUps(orgId)` ã Auto-detected items pending reply/quote/payment/delivery.
- `extractPurchaseIntent(conversationId)` ã Structured `{ category, priceRange, confidence }` from free text.
- `extractLeadInsights(phone)` ã Score, vertical, inferred budget, urgency, best channel.
- `searchBusinessMemory(query)` ã Semantic vector search across the entire business memory.
- `getCustomerRelationshipSummary(phone)` ã LTV, frequency, favorites, churn risk, upsell signals.
- `extractOrdersFromConversation(conversationId)` ã Structured orders (SKU, qty, amount, address) from chat text.

## Operational tools (the boring-but-necessary)

- `sendMessage`, `sendTemplate`, `sendMedia` ã WhatsApp messaging respecting opt-out and 24h window.
- `createOrder`, `confirmOrder`, `cancelOrder` ã Order lifecycle.
- `createPaymentLink`, `generateSpeiInstructions`, `getPaymentStatus` ã Mexican payment rails.
- `generateInvoice` ã CFDI 4.0 stamping via Facturapi.
- `handoffToHuman` ã Escalation with structured summary, urgency, reason.
- `updateConsent`, `getConsent` ã LFPDPPP-compliant opt-in/out with audit evidence.

All tools are **scoped, idempotent, audited**. No tool can mark a payment as `paid` ã only verified PSP webhooks or authenticated human operators can.

## Why LATAM-specific

LATAM businesses **live on WhatsApp**: in Mexico, 85% of SMBs sell, collect, and serve through it. This requires capabilities global infrastructure doesn't have natively:

- **Mûˋxico:** SAT workflows, CFDI 4.0 invoicing, RFC validation, SPEI, OXXO, LFPDPPP compliance, ARCO rights, regional Spanish.
- **Argentina:** AFIP-compatible invoicing roadmap, MercadoPago AR rails, espaûÝol rioplatense.
- **Colombia:** PSE rails, espaûÝol colombiano.
- **Brasil (next):** PIX, NFe, portuguûˆs brasileiro, LGPD.

## Stacks supported

- **MCP-native:** Claude Desktop, Claude Code, Cursor, OpenAI Agents (via adapter), any MCP client.
- **OpenAI Agents / Responses API:** tools as functions or auto-adapted from MCP.
- **LangChain / LangGraph:** plug-and-play tool node; TramsTec replaces ChatMessageHistory for WhatsApp.
- **n8n / Make / Zapier:** HTTP nodes with bearer auth, no-code flows.
- **Custom agents:** TypeScript SDK (`@tramstec/sdk`), Python SDK, plain REST.
- **Vertical AI SaaS:** white-label, multi-tenant, scoped API keys per client, optional revenue share.

## Pricing (usage-based, not per-user)

- **Developer** ã Free forever. 10k tool calls + 5k memory ops + 100 conversations/mo. 1 org.
- **Builder** ã $29 USD/mo ($499 MXN). 100k tool calls + 50k memory ops + 1k conversations/mo. 1 org, 1 WhatsApp.
- **Agency** ã $129 USD/mo ($2,499 MXN). 500k tool calls + 250k memory ops + 5k conversations/mo. Up to 5 orgs.
- **Platform** ã $499 USD/mo ($9,999 MXN). 5M tool calls + 1M memory ops + 25k conversations/mo. White-label, unlimited orgs, 99.9% SLA.
- **Enterprise** ã Custom. Dedicated infra / on-prem, data residency MX/LATAM, 99.95% SLA + 24/7, MSA/NDA/DPA.

Overage: $0.003 USD per extra tool call, $0.001 USD per extra memory op. WhatsApp/Meta and payment processor fees pass through at cost. CFDI 4.0 available for MX customers.

## Transport

- **HTTP/SSE** (production, multi-tenant): `https://mcp.tramstec.com/sse` with `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`.
- **stdio** (single-tenant, dev): `WCM_ORG_ID=<org_id> wcm-mcp`.
- **REST** (clients without MCP): `https://api.tramstec.com/v1/tools/:name` with bearer auth.

## Infrastructure

- 99.95% uptime objective. Multi-AZ by default. Failover automated.
- Vector search on pgvector (HNSW), p95 < 50ms on semantic queries.
- Memory durability: Postgres + WAL streaming, 90 days hot, 730 days archived, exportable.
- Event-driven: BullMQ + Redis, retries with exponential backoff, dead-letter queues.
- Observability: Sentry, OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, structured logs.
- Security: scoped API keys, immutable audit log, encryption at rest + transit, SOC2 in roadmap.

## Pages

- [/](https://tramstec.com/) ã Landing (Spanish, LATAM-first positioning).
- [/en/](https://tramstec.com/en/) ã English version (developer/AI-platform positioning).
- [/#tools](https://tramstec.com/#tools) ã AI-native tools catalog.
- [/#agentes](https://tramstec.com/#agentes) ã Supported agent stacks.
- [/#latam](https://tramstec.com/#latam) ã LATAM business intelligence.
- [/#precios](https://tramstec.com/#precios) ã Pricing (Developer ã Enterprise).
- [/#empezar](https://tramstec.com/#empezar) ã Waitlist / sign-up.
- [/mcp-manifest.json](https://tramstec.com/mcp-manifest.json) ã Machine-readable MCP server descriptor.
- [/.well-known/ai-plugin.json](https://tramstec.com/.well-known/ai-plugin.json) ã OpenAI plugin convention.

## Compliance and safety

- **LFPDPPP** (Mexico data protection) compliant; ARCO rights endpoints.
- **WhatsApp Business Policy** enforced: opt-out is a one-message kill switch; templates require approval and explicit opt-in.
- **No payment auto-confirmation** ã only verified PSP webhooks or authenticated human operators can mark payments as `paid`.
- **Every tool call** is audited with timestamp, actor, args, result, latency.
- **Multi-tenant isolation** via Postgres RLS + defensive checks in each query path.
